description: This figure includes two plots exploring mechanical properties of austenite-based Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels. The first plot presents the effect of Al content (3 to 10 wt%) on properties of Fe-30Mn-xAl-(0.85, 0.95)C steels, which were water quenched from 1150 °C and aged at 550 °C for 16 h. It plots ultimate tensile strength (UTS, triangular markers), yield strength (YS, open triangular markers), U-notch Charpy impact energy (E_CUN, diamond markers), total elongation (TE, solid circular markers) and δ-ferrite fraction (cross markers) for both 0.95 wt% C (solid lines) and 0.85 wt% C (dashed lines) specimens. The second plot shows the effect of C content (0.4 to 1.2 wt%) on Fe-30Mn-9Al-xC steel properties, with dashed lines for 1050 °C water quenched samples and solid lines for samples aged at 550 °C for 16 h. Key trends: UTS and YS continuously increase when Al content exceeds 7 wt% and C content rises; E_CUN and TE reach their peaks at ~6 wt% Al and ~0.7 wt% C respectively, then decrease at higher Al/C content, while δ-ferrite is only detected at Al >8 wt% and reduces with increasing C. This figure supports that aged Fe-Mn-Al-C alloys with Al >7 wt% and C >0.7 wt% achieve significant strengthening from dispersed nano-sized κ-carbide precipitates, accompanied by reduced ductility and impact toughness at higher alloying levels.
